_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 5-5 Adult Chest Pain/Acute Coronary Syndrome (Revised 11/17/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Cardiac Protocol Section 5-5: Chest Pain/Acute Coronary Syndrome — MDHHS Approved 11/17/25, Reviewed 2025
purpose: Goal is to reduce cardiac workload and maximize myocardial oxygen delivery by reducing anxiety, appropriately oxygenating, and relieving pain. For non-cardiac causes of chest pain, refer to appropriate protocol which may include Pain Management-Procedure Protocol.

sections.title: Chest Pain / ACS Treatment
points: Obtain 12-lead ECG as early as possible WITHOUT delaying medication administration (per MCA selection: BLS or Specialist — follow 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol)
Administer oxygen 4 L/min by nasal cannula if SpO2 < 94%
Assist patient in use of their own aspirin up to a dose of 325 mg per formulation (chew, swallow, etc.)
Administer aspirin up to 325 mg PO — chew and swallow — if no aspirin or suspected insufficient dose since onset of chest pain (per MCA selection may be MFR and/or EMT skill)
Inquire of ALL patients regardless of identified gender if they have taken an erectile dysfunction medication or medications used to treat pulmonary hypertension in the last 48 hours
If ED/pulmonary hypertension medication taken in last 48 hours: DO NOT ADMINISTER/ASSIST WITH NITROGLYCERIN — contact Medical Control
Consider fentanyl EARLY when nitroglycerin is contraindicated due to ED medication
If no ED medication, SBP > 120 mmHg, and patient has own prescribed nitroglycerin available (check expiration): assist patient in use of their own nitroglycerin up to maximum 3 doses
Prior to IV establishment, if no ED medication and SBP > 120 mmHg: administer nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL up to maximum 3 doses (per MCA selection may be EMT skill)
Start IV NS or LR KVO per Vascular Access and IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol

If SBP < 100 mmHg: administer 250 mL fluid bolus; may repeat up to 3 times for total of 1 liter — between boluses assess patient response and monitor for pulmonary edema; if pulmonary edema noted, stop fluids and contact Medical Control
With IV established, no ED medication, SBP > 100 mmHg: administer nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL; may repeat every 3–5 minutes while chest pain persists and SBP > 100 mmHg
Obtain 12-lead ECG (per MCA selection: BLS or Specialist); if positive for acute STEMI: follow local MCA transport protocol and alert hospital as soon as possible

For suspected cardiac chest pain refractory to nitroglycerin, OR nitroglycerin is contraindicated due to ED medication: administer fentanyl 1 mcg/kg IV/IO/IM/IN — rounded doses: ≤ 25 kg = 25 mcg; 26–50 kg = 50 mcg; 51–75 kg = 75 mcg; ≥ 76 kg = 100 mcg; IV/IO/IN may repeat ONE TIME (total dose must not exceed 200 mcg); if IM administration — may NOT repeat

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select provider level authorized to obtain 12-lead ECG
MCA must select which provider levels may administer aspirin 325 mg PO for chest pain
MCA may authorize EMT to administer nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL (prior to IV, up to 3 doses, SBP > 120) for chest pain/ACS

key_drugs.name: Aspirin 325 mg PO (ACS)
key_drugs.dose: Up to 325 mg PO — chew and swallow
key_drugs.indication: Suspected ACS/cardiac chest pain — if no aspirin or suspected insufficient dose since onset
key_drugs.contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to aspirin; active GI bleeding
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — MFR and/or EMT options available
key_drugs.notes: Assist patient with their own aspirin first. Administer EMS aspirin if no aspirin or insufficient dose received since symptom onset.

key_drugs.name: Nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL (ACS)
key_drugs.dose: 0.4 mg SL — repeat every 3–5 minutes; pre-IV max 3 doses (SBP > 120); with IV continue while SBP > 100 and chest pain persists
key_drugs.indication: Cardiac chest pain — SBP > 120 (pre-IV) or SBP > 100 (with IV); no ED/PH medications in last 48 hours
key_drugs.contraindications: ED medications (sildenafil, tadalafil, vardenafil, avanafil) or pulmonary hypertension medications in last 48 hours — DO NOT ADMINISTER; SBP < 120 (pre-IV); SBP < 100 (with IV); RV infarction (right-sided STEMI)
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT option available (pre-IV); Specialist for IV-access dosing
key_drugs.notes: ALWAYS inquire about ED/pulmonary hypertension medications regardless of patient's identified gender. Assist with patient's own nitro first if prescribed and available.

key_drugs.name: Fentanyl IV/IO/IM/IN (Refractory Cardiac Pain)
key_drugs.dose: 1 mcg/kg IV/IO/IM/IN — weight-based rounded doses: ≤ 25 kg = 25 mcg; 26–50 kg = 50 mcg; 51–75 kg = 75 mcg; ≥ 76 kg = 100 mcg; IV/IO/IN: may repeat ONCE (max total 200 mcg); IM: may NOT repeat
key_drugs.indication: Cardiac chest pain refractory to nitroglycerin, OR nitroglycerin contraindicated due to ED medication
key_drugs.contraindications: Hemodynamic instability (SBP < 100) — use with caution; hypersensitivity
key_drugs.notes: Consider fentanyl EARLY when nitroglycerin is contraindicated. Maximum total IV/IO/IN dose 200 mcg. IM route — single dose only, no repeat.

deterioration_flags: STEMI on 12-lead ECG — immediate hospital notification; follow MCA STEMI transport protocol
ED/pulmonary hypertension medication in last 48 hours — DO NOT give nitroglycerin; contact Medical Control; consider fentanyl for pain
SBP < 100 mmHg — IV fluid boluses 250 mL x up to 4 (max 1L); stop if pulmonary edema develops; contact Medical Control
Chest pain unresponsive to nitroglycerin — fentanyl 1 mcg/kg (rounded per weight table); maximum 200 mcg total IV/IO/IN
Pulmonary edema develops — stop fluid; refer to Pulmonary Edema/Cardiogenic Shock protocol
scope_notes: SpO2 monitoring, oxygen via NC, assisting patient with own aspirin and own nitroglycerin: all levels
12-lead ECG: per MCA selection — BLS or Specialist
Aspirin 325 mg PO: per MCA selection — MFR and/or EMT options available
Nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL (pre-IV): per MCA selection — EMT option available
IV access, nitroglycerin (with IV): Specialist skill
